records.ety: [Icel. reið a riding, raid; akin to E. road. See Road a way.]

defs: A hostile or predatory incursion; an inroad or incursion of mounted men; a sudden and rapid invasion by a cavalry force; a foray.
quotes: Marauding chief! his sole delight The moonlight raid, the morning fight.
There are permanent conquests, temporary occupations, and occasional raids.
notes: ☞ A Scottish word which came into common use in the United States during the Civil War, and was soon extended in its application.

defs: An attack or invasion for the purpose of making arrests, seizing property, or plundering; as, a raid of the police upon a gambling house; a raid of contractors on the public treasury.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Raided; p. pr. & vb. n. Raiding.]

defs: To make a raid upon or into; as, two regiments raided the border counties.
